duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: forceInclusion only enforces block-based delay check, completely omitting the time-based delay check despite ForceIncludeTimeTooSoon error being defined and delaySeconds being stored
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical missing timestamp check in SequencerInbox.forceInclusion. They reference the same function, same code lines, same unused ForceIncludeTimeTooSoon error, and same exploitation path. TECH-H001 selected for most comprehensive technical analysis with precise line references and HIGH severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Staking pool depositBalance mapping is never decremented when tokens leave the pool via createAssertion/createEdge, enabling old depositors to steal new deposits after stake slashing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eleven threats describe the same AbsBoldStakingPool accounting flaw: depositBalance not adjusted on protocol moves, enabling deposit theft after slashing. TECH2-H005 here refers to the 'Pool-level token theft' variant. ECON2-H001 selected as CRITICAL with the most comprehensive description covering both free-riding and post-slash theft, plus detailed attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless forceInclusion calls buffer.update before delay check, creating self-reinforcing depletion feedback loop and enabling strategic buffer state manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same issue: buffer.update called before the delay check in forceInclusion, allowing the update to reduce effective delay and enabling strategic buffer manipulation by permissionless callers. ECON-H003 and OPS-H003 are broader framings of the same mechanism. TECH-H002 selected for most precise technical description of the feedback loop.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: First layer zero edge stake held in EdgeChallengeManager contract is permanently locked if that edge loses, with no recovery mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the same asymmetric stake routing: first edge stake goes to address(this) while rivals go to excessStakeReceiver, and refundStake requires confirmed status. TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'First Edge Stake Permanently Locked' variant. ECON2-H002 selected for best economic impact analysis and clear description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: isValidBufferConfig allows replenishRateInBasis=0, creating permanent buffer depletion with no recovery after any sequencer delay
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same config validation gap: replenishRateInBasis=0 passes validation but makes buffer recovery impossible. Same functions, same code pattern. TECH-H007 selected for most detailed technical analysis of the validation gap.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DelayBuffer.calcBuffer integer division truncation causes replenishment to round to zero for frequent batch posting, permanently preventing buffer recovery
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the same integer division rounding issue in calcBuffer where (elapsed * replenishRateInBasis) / BASIS truncates to zero. TECH2-H004 here refers to the 'Delay Buffer Replenishment Stagnation' variant. ECON-H010 selected as HIGH severity with key insight about prevBlockNumber being updated unconditionally, discarding fractional progress.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BOLDUpgradeAction.cleanupOldRollup caps staker refunds at 50 and skips challenged stakers, potentially leaving remaining stakers' funds locked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same bounded iteration in cleanupOldRollup. TECH2-H007 here refers to the 'Staker Funds Temporarily Locked' variant. TECH-H006 selected as HIGH severity and covers both the 50-staker cap AND the challenge filter, plus dependency on IMPL_PATCHED_OLD_ROLLUP_USER.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RollupCreator._deployFactories ETH refund via raw call to msg.sender enables reentrancy and DoS of rollup creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same external call to untrusted msg.sender during _deployFactories ETH refund. TECH2-H004 here refers to the 'RollupCreator._deployFactories ETH refund uses raw call' variant. Selected as HIGH severity with most comprehensive reentrancy analysis including nested deployment risks.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RollupCreator refunds address(this).balance (entire contract balance) to caller, allowing sweeping of accidentally sent or accumulated ETH
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: _deployFactories sends the entire contract balance as refund, not just excess from current deployment. ECON2-H009 selected for more detailed description including receive() function accumulation mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EdgeStakingPool contracts cannot create edges when validator whitelist is enabled because pool contracts are not whitelisted
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same whitelist enforcement blocking permissionlessly-deployed pool contracts from creating edges. OPS2-H005 selected for more specific proof_of_signal line references and detailed preconditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SequencerInbox.updateRollupAddress requires old rollup owner authorization, which can become inaccessible if rollup pointer becomes stale during migration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same authorization deadlock in updateRollupAddress. TECH2-H007 here refers to the 'SequencerInbox.updateRollupAddress may be permanently blocked' variant. OPS-H015 selected for more comprehensive analysis of downstream admin function impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Blob gas cost normalization (blobCost / block.basefee) truncates to zero when basefee is zero or significantly larger than blob cost, denying batch poster reimbursement
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same integer division issue in formBlobDataHash. ECON-H017 focuses on zero basefee case; ECON2-H006 covers the broader truncation scenario. ECON2-H006 selected for more general and comprehensive analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DelayBuffer.isSynced subtraction underflows when prevBlockNumber exceeds block.number (after chain fork or bad initialization), bricking batch posting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same unchecked subtraction in isSynced that can revert under Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic. ECON-H009 selected for more specific chain fork scenario with better proof_of_signal connecting removeDelayAfterFork not resetting buffer state.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Deterministic CREATE2 staking pool deployment with salt=0 enables permissionless address squatting and misleading pool discovery
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same permissionless CREATE2 pool deployment squatting risk. OPS-H013 selected for slightly more detailed operational analysis including pre-positioning for the depositBalance theft scenario.
